decisions: Kept Phase 1 scope narrow: pure parser/classifier plus basic `watch` command, without adding daemon/process-monitor scaffolding from later phases.
Used event-driven `awaiting_tool_result` as the minimal proxy for “tokens frozen / API still pending” in the MVP classifier.
Verified deliverable with a synthetic session log instead of waiting on a live Claude session.
lessons: ApiHang should only trigger while a tool result is still outstanding; otherwise long user silence becomes a false positive.
Session log auto-discovery must filter for actual Claude session logs, not arbitrary repo `.jsonl` files like `.beads/issues.jsonl`.
Claude compact events can appear as `type=system` with `subtype=compact_boundary`, so parser logic should not rely only on top-level `type`.
fields.result_summary: success - saw watch detects ApiHang and prints alert
warnings: Phase 1 epic still has many bead dependencies open in `.beads/issues.jsonl`, but the requested deliverable is now met by the current MVP implementation.
Current `watch` command is still one-shot over a file snapshot; real-time tailing/process monitoring remains future work.

artifacts: Defined documented, serializable core types in `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:16`
Updated session parsing to emit `ToolCall` records with command extraction in `crates/saw-core/src/session.rs:1`
decisions: Kept `AgentPhase` variants exactly as requested, using `Duration` payloads only for `ApiHang` and `Idle`, and unit variants for the rest.

Expanded `AgentState` beyond the MVP fields to include the tracking fields described in the plan (`recent_tool_calls`, file/process timing, scope state, cached phase) while staying pure-data and serde-friendly.
lessons: A tuple-style `AgentEvent::ToolCall(ToolCall)` keeps the event model cleaner and makes shared metadata reusable across parser/state-machine code.
`cargo doc --no-deps` completed cleanly, so the inline docs are sufficient for current rustdoc generation.
fields.result_summary: success - implemented saw-core core data structures
warnings: `AgentPhase::ToolLoop`, `TestLoop`, `TaskBlocked`, and `ScopeLeaking` are intentionally unit variants right now; detailed payloads can be layered later through `LoopScore` and `AgentState` without changing this task’s requested shape.

artifacts: Added loop detector unit tests in `crates/saw-core/src/loop_detector.rs:157`
Updated session parsing to populate richer tool call/result fields needed by current core types in `crates/saw-core/src/session.rs:10`
decisions: Used `ToolCall.is_write` plus known write-capable tool names as the rewrite signal, while still requiring a file path.
Counted consecutive test failures by walking backward from the newest in-window call and stopping on the first non-failed-test call.
Treated test commands conservatively via common command patterns (`cargo test`, `pytest`, `go test`, `npm/pnpm/yarn/bun test`).
lessons: The current `saw-core` types had evolved beyond the earlier parser stubs, so `session.rs` needed alignment for `is_write`, sidechain flags, stderr/interrupted handling, and persisted-output parsing before tests would compile.
The 5-minute sliding window is anchored to the latest observed tool call, so older writes correctly fall out of the rewrite count even when the same file appears earlier in history.
fields.result_summary: success - implemented saw-core loop detection with 5-minute sliding window scoring and unit tests
warnings: `compute_loop_score` now works and is tested, but classifier integration for emitting `AgentPhase::ToolLoop` / `AgentPhase::TestLoop` is still separate work.
A `Blocking waiting for file lock on artifact directory` message appeared during cargo runs, but the final full `cargo test -p saw-core` passed cleanly.

decisions: Kept auto-detection inside `watch.rs` for now instead of inventing a new abstraction.
Used `System::refresh_processes(ProcessesToUpdate::Some(&[pid]), true)` + `system.process(pid)` for liveness.
Returned a clear error when no alive sessions remain in `~/.claude/sessions`.
Resolved the selected `SessionFile` to `.claude/projects/<slug>/<sessionId>.jsonl` using slug-encoded `cwd`.
lessons: `~/.claude/sessions/*.json` is enough to drive PID discovery reliably when filtered through `sysinfo`.
Matching by canonicalized `cwd` gives the right session for the current project; otherwise highest `startedAt` is a good fallback.
The branch had unrelated `saw-core` parser drift; I fixed the missing sidechain/persisted-output fields so tests could pass cleanly.
fields.result_summary: success - implemented PID auto-detection for `saw watch`
warnings: `cargo run -- watch --timeout-secs 1` auto-detected a live Claude session and selected a `membrain` session because there is currently no live `~/.claude/sessions/*.json` entry for this repo path.
`crates/saw-core/src/session.rs` had pre-existing incomplete changes; they are now brought into a compiling state as part of this task.

artifacts: Verified metrics helpers already implemented in `crates/saw-core/src/metrics.rs:11`
Verified unit coverage for requested helpers in `crates/saw-core/src/metrics.rs:252`
decisions: Kept existing implementation unchanged since it already matches the task:
`compute_silence` takes the max of `last_jsonl_record_at`, `last_file_event_at`, and `last_hook_event_at`, with fallback behavior at `crates/saw-core/src/metrics.rs:11`
`TokenTracker` with `update()`/`is_frozen()` exists at `crates/saw-core/src/metrics.rs:40`
`activity_weight()` exists at `crates/saw-core/src/metrics.rs:88`
`is_bash_failure()` covers the 4 requested signal classes at `crates/saw-core/src/metrics.rs:169`
Unit tests cover each requested function starting at `crates/saw-core/src/metrics.rs:252`
lessons: Changed approach from editing-first to verify-first.
Concrete proof step: ran targeted metrics tests before making changes.
No code changes were needed because the requested functionality was already present and passing.
warnings: `compute_silence` currently uses `DateTime<Utc>` rather than the task text’s `Instant`; behavior is correct for this codebase’s clock domain, and tests pass.

decisions: Extended `AgentEvent` payloads and `ToolCall` to include `is_sidechain`, and extended `ToolResult` to carry `output`, `stderr`, `interrupted`, and `persisted_output_path`.

Kept `SessionRecord::parse(line: &str) -> Option<AgentEvent>` strict and panic-free: malformed or partial JSON returns `None`, while non-signal record types like `progress` and `file-history-snapshot` are ignored.
Reused the existing tuple-style `AgentEvent::ToolCall(ToolCall)` model and updated `loop_detector` fixtures to match the richer `ToolCall` schema.
lessons: Claude assistant records may produce either `ToolCall` or `TokenActivity`; token usage should only be emitted when no `tool_use` item exists in the assistant content array.
`<persisted-output>` parsing is safest when scanning past the `Full output saved to:` marker and skipping blank/preview lines.
Sidechain awareness needed to be carried into core event structs so later metrics/classifier code can weight subagent activity correctly.
fields.result_summary: success - implemented resilient saw-core JSONL parser in `crates/saw-core/src/session.rs`
warnings: “Partial lines are buffered correctly” is satisfied at the parser contract level by returning `None` for incomplete JSON; actual newline buffering still belongs to the future JSONL tailer task, not `session.rs`.

decisions: Created a new `crates/saw-daemon` crate now because the task explicitly targeted `saw-daemon/event_bus.rs` and the workspace did not contain that crate yet.
Counted dropped events when a subscriber observes `Lagged`, which matches Tokio broadcast semantics for slow-consumer loss.
Ignored `send()` receiver-count errors in `publish()` while still incrementing `events_published`, since publishing should remain fire-and-forget.
lessons: Tokio broadcast lag behavior is consumer-visible through `RecvError::Lagged`; the sender stays non-blocking and the receiver must account for dropped events.
Broadcast retention is implementation-defined enough that tests should assert lag semantics and configured capacity, not assume an exact first-retained index from raw capacity math.
warnings: Warning logging is wired through `log::warn!`; runtime log output depends on the eventual daemon binary initializing a logger.
`events_dropped` reflects lag observed by subscribers, so it is aggregate observed loss, not a per-publish global drop signal.

artifacts: Expanded state and phase payloads in `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:16` and `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:343`
Hooked write-driven scope tracking into state updates in `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:368`
Added action enums, guard handling, checkpoint save, and kill/bell actions in `src/cmd/watch.rs:13`, `src/cmd/watch.rs:49`, and `src/cmd/watch.rs:284`
Added verification tests for classifier and watch command in `crates/saw-core/src/lib.rs:77` and `src/cmd/watch.rs:568`
Synthetic verification logs used for manual CLI checks: `/tmp/saw_tool_loop.jsonl`, `/tmp/saw_test_loop.jsonl`, `/tmp/saw_scope_leak.jsonl`
decisions: Kept Phase 2 scoped to the current one-shot `watch` architecture instead of adding daemon/tailing infrastructure from later phases.
Reused `compute_loop_score` plus command-pattern matching in the classifier for ToolLoop/TestLoop detection rather than inventing a second loop-analysis path.
Implemented `checkpoint-kill` as a local `.saw/checkpoints/<timestamp>/` snapshot with copied modified files, `saw-state.json`, `session-snapshot.jsonl`, and `manifest.json`.
Applied scope guard checks against normalized absolute guard paths in the CLI so `--guard src/auth` works predictably from the project root.
lessons: The repo state had already advanced beyond the original Phase 1 MVP, so the safest path was to extend existing `loop_detector`, `metrics`, and richer parser/types instead of rebuilding from the earlier minimal snapshot.
Scope leak detection can work in one-shot mode by treating write-like tool calls as file modifications during state replay, even before a real filesystem watcher exists.

`cargo test` at workspace root is currently blocked by a missing `crates/saw-daemon` workspace member in `Cargo.toml`; targeted manifest-specific test/check commands are needed until the workspace is fixed.
fields.result_summary: success - saw watch now detects ApiHang, ToolLoop, TestLoop, and ScopeLeaking; supports --on-stuck/--on-scope-leak actions, --guard, and checkpoint save
warnings: `watch` is still a one-shot snapshot command, not a live tailing/process-monitor loop; this satisfies the phase deliverable in current architecture but not the eventual real-time design.

Root workspace commands still fail because `Cargo.toml` references missing member `crates/saw-daemon`; I verified Phase 2 with `cargo test --manifest-path .../crates/saw-core/Cargo.toml`, `cargo check --manifest-path .../Cargo.toml --bin saw`, `cargo test --manifest-path .../Cargo.toml --bin saw`, and synthetic CLI runs.
I did not modify `.beads/issues.jsonl`; epic/task closure state may still need separate Grove/bead bookkeeping outside this code deliverable.

decisions: Reused the existing `EventBus` + `AgentState::apply(AgentEvent::FileModified)` path instead of inventing a parallel guard-leak signal.
Kept guard violations as normal file events; the already-implemented classifier/state logic turns them into `AgentPhase::ScopeLeaking`.
Filtered out folder-only create/remove events and tracked file events only.
lessons: The repo already had `process_monitor.rs`; `lib.rs` needed to export watcher alongside it.
Existing state/classifier code already supports watcher-driven scope leak detection, so watcher only needed to normalize and publish events correctly.
warnings: `watcher.rs` is implemented and tested, but not yet integrated into the CLI runtime loop in `src/cmd/watch.rs`; current work delivers the daemon-side watcher module requested.
`notify` backends can coalesce low-level FS events differently by platform, so tests assert semantic detection rather than exact raw notify sequences.

decisions: Used a concrete verification path first: ran the targeted `process_monitor` tests before attempting further edits.
No code changes were needed because the requested functionality was already present and passing.
lessons: The codebase’s event model does not use a separate `AgentEvent::Dead`; liveness is encoded in `ProcessMetrics.process_alive` from `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:142`.
warnings: Test/build output includes unrelated unused-import warnings in `crates/saw-daemon/src/watcher.rs:7` and `crates/saw-daemon/src/watcher.rs:167`.

decisions: Kept the existing notify + interval hybrid approach because it satisfies low-latency delivery while still recovering from missed/creation/rotation events.
Fixed only the compile blockers needed to validate the JSONL tailer:
`state.guard_path` → `state.guard_paths` in `crates/saw-daemon/src/watcher.rs:251`
`scope_leak_state.guard_path` → `guard_paths` in `crates/saw-core/src/lib.rs:155`
`guard: None` → `guard: Vec::new()` in `src/cmd/watch.rs:748`
lessons: The requested tailer was already present; the actual blocker was unrelated compile drift in tests.
Changed verification path after the first failure: fixed stale `guard_path`/`guard` references, then re-ran a single concrete JSONL tail test before expanding to broader suites.
warnings: There is still a pre-existing warning: unused method `mark_jsonl_activity` in `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:358`. Tests pass despite it.

artifacts: Added per-sample I/O rate calculation from `Process::disk_usage()`.
Added tests for rate calculation and dead-event zero-rate behavior.
State now records last non-zero I/O activity timestamp.
Added unit test for zero/non-zero I/O activity tracking.
Added unit tests for rate calculation and 60s zero-I/O window behavior.
`ApiHang` now requires both API silence and no recent I/O activity.
This makes zero I/O for >60s a secondary stuck signal.
Added classifier test proving recent I/O suppresses false ApiHang.
Plain-text status output now includes `io_read` and `io_write` rates.
decisions: Used `delta bytes / elapsed seconds` for rate calculation with saturating subtraction.
Kept first sample rates at `0.0` because there is no previous baseline.
Tracked `last_io_activity_at` only when either I/O rate is non-zero.
Treated I/O as a secondary signal for stuck detection by gating `ApiHang` on both silence and I/O inactivity.
Interpreted “metrics panel” using the current plain-text watch status output, since this repo does not yet contain a TUI metrics panel implementation.
lessons: The repo already tracked cumulative I/O bytes in `process_monitor`, so the safe path was to extend that existing event flow instead of introducing a new observer.
Running multiple `cargo test` commands in parallel caused noisy failures/cancellations due to shared artifact locking; sequential targeted runs were the reliable verification path.
A pre-existing guard-selection edge case surfaced during testing and was corrected by preferring the most specific matching guard candidate.
warnings: The acceptance item “metrics panel shows I/O rate” is satisfied in the current CLI watch output, not a TUI panel, because no TUI/metrics-panel code exists in this repo yet.
One test run briefly waited on a Cargo artifact lock; subsequent sequential runs passed.

decisions: Kept scope-leak detection on the existing `AgentState::apply(AgentEvent::FileModified)` and classifier path rather than adding a parallel detector.
Preserved `--guard` as clap `Vec<PathBuf>` with `value_delimiter=','`, which already supports comma-separated guards like `--guard src/auth/,tests/auth/`.
For multiple guards, report the most specific non-matching guard by longest shared prefix and deeper path length, so the emitted `guard_path` is more informative than always using the first guard.
Updated `saw-daemon::FileWatcher` to accept `Vec<PathBuf>` so watcher-side logging and tests align with multi-guard behavior.
Fixed an unrelated compile blocker in `crates/saw-daemon/src/process_monitor.rs` (`&disk_usage`) so daemon tests could run.
lessons: The guard-path feature was partially implemented already; the fastest path was fixing stale field/test drift and proving behavior with focused tests instead of reworking the CLI surface.
Exact test names for binary and library crates required `cargo test -- --list` first; that avoided repeating filtered-out “0 tests” runs.
fields.result_summary: success - implemented multi-guard scope leak detection with violating file tracking and scope leak actions
warnings: `ScopeLeaking` still stores a single `guard_path` in the phase payload, so multi-guard reporting is “best matching guard”, not a full set of candidate guards.

artifacts: Updated `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:255`-`290` to keep separate main vs sidechain JSONL timestamps and track `subagent_count`.
Updated `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:377`-`518` so sidechain events no longer arm or clear main-session `awaiting_tool_result`, while still recording sidechain activity.
Added classifier regression test for sidechain silence in `crates/saw-core/src/lib.rs:102`-`123`.
decisions: Kept monitoring scoped to the parent JSONL only, matching the current Phase 5 note.
Treated sidechain activity as informational for scoring, but prevented it from independently arming or resolving `ApiHang`.
Left TUI work untouched because there is no TUI implementation in this checkout yet, and the task marked it optional.
lessons: The parser was already correct; the real bug was in state application where sidechain events were mutating parent-session hang state.
For this repo, “subagent_count” is currently an approximate sidechain activity count, not distinct subagent cardinality, because parent JSONL only exposes `isSidechain` and not a stable subagent ID.
warnings: `subagent_count` is not exact distinct-agent count with the current JSONL schema.
No `subagents/<agent>.jsonl` monitoring was added in this pass.

artifacts: Added shared CLI/session snapshot utilities in `src/cmd/common.rs`
decisions: Chose a thin implementation path: hook events persist to `.saw/hooks/<session>.jsonl` and are merged into snapshots, instead of building a Unix socket daemon first.
Kept TUI minimal but functional: poll-based dashboard with ratatui, status/file/metrics/alerts panels, and key actions `q`, `k`, `K`, `c`.
Implemented robot output on `watch` as structured JSON snapshots/events rather than a fully streamed daemon protocol.
lessons: The previous failure mode was a compile blocker in `src/cmd/watch.rs` tests (`guard: None` vs `Vec<PathBuf>`); fixing that first unblocked the rest of Phase 3 work.
A concrete one-test probe was enough to reveal baseline breakage before expanding scope.
`saw status --json` works even with no live local session by returning a `Dead` snapshot instead of crashing.
Synthetic hook verification via stdin is a fast proof path for hook mode without needing a live Claude hook roundtrip.
warnings: Hook transport is file-backed, not Unix-socket-backed as originally envisioned in `PLAN.md`.
`saw status` currently exits `1` with a valid JSON `Dead` snapshot when no active session is detected.
Phase 3 bead children in `.beads/issues.jsonl` still appear open; code is implemented, but bead status metadata was not updated.

decisions: Added the requested tests as an integration test file at `tests/metrics_tests.rs` instead of modifying the existing internal unit tests in `crates/saw-core/src/metrics.rs:253`.
Kept `is_bash_failure()` coverage aligned to the acceptance criteria’s 4 signal classes: `is_error`, non-empty `stderr`, `interrupted`, and content-pattern failure text.
lessons: `TokenTracker::is_frozen()` can be tested through public API by constructing trackers with past `Instant`s, avoiding access to private helper methods.
`compute_silence()` intentionally prioritizes visible activity sources (`last_jsonl_record_at`, `last_file_event_at`, `last_hook_event_at`) over `last_event_at`.
warnings: The repo already has overlapping in-module unit tests for some of these helpers in `crates/saw-core/src/metrics.rs:274`; this new file duplicates some coverage intentionally to satisfy the task requirement to create `tests/metrics_tests.rs`.

artifacts: Added standalone loop-detector integration tests in `tests/loop_detector_tests.rs:1`
decisions: Used `classify(...)` from `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:31` in the new tests so the acceptance criteria map to actual `ToolLoop`/`TestLoop` behavior, not just raw scores.
Interpreted “Test failures: 3+ consecutive failures → ToolLoop” as the implemented `AgentPhase::TestLoop` behavior in `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:63`.
Interpreted “rate limiting works” as validating the capped `recent_tool_calls` window rather than inventing new production code.
lessons: The repo already had inline unit tests in `crates/saw-core/src/loop_detector.rs:132`, but this task explicitly wanted a standalone test file.

There is no separate “rate limiting” API in the loop detector; the closest implemented mechanism is the bounded recent-tool-call history in `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:13` and `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:485`, which keeps loop scoring cheap.
warnings: The requested location said `tests/loop_detector_tests.rs`; because the root package already depends on `saw-core`, that file runs as an integration test against the published crate API, not as an internal unit test module.
Existing inline loop-detector tests remain in `crates/saw-core/src/loop_detector.rs:132`, so coverage is now duplicated by design.

artifacts: `Cargo.toml:21` — added `dev-dependencies` for `saw-daemon` and `tokio`, plus `[[test]]` entry for `jsonl_tail`
file rotation to newest JSONL without event loss
decisions: Kept tests external to the crate as requested instead of extending `crates/saw-daemon/src/jsonl_tail.rs:292`.
Used temp directories and real file appends with a live `JsonlTailer` task to exercise actual file reads and notify/poll behavior.
Verified large-file behavior by asserting all 1200 emitted events are received, which checks streaming correctness without introducing memory instrumentation.
lessons: Root integration tests needed an explicit `[[test]]` target because the requested file lives under `tests/integration/jsonl_tail.rs` instead of Cargo’s default `tests/*.rs`.
`ToolResult.stderr` is `None` when the JSON omits the field, so fixtures should omit empty stderr rather than serialize `\"stderr\":\"\"`.
warnings: The acceptance criteria requested `1000+` records; the test uses 1200.
These tests validate observable behavior, not internal memory allocations, so “without memory issues” is covered indirectly via successful large-batch processing.

artifacts: Updated phase classification in `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:31` to follow the requested priority order: `Dead > ScopeLeaking > ContextReset > ToolLoop > TestLoop > ApiHang > Idle > Thinking > Working`
Tightened `ApiHang` detection in `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:88` to require all requested signals: outstanding tool wait, silence > 120s, CPU < 1%, no recent I/O, and frozen token activity

Added focused classifier transition tests in `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:222` covering dead, scope leak, context reset, tool loop, test loop, api hang, idle, thinking, working, and edge-case non-panicking behavior
decisions: Kept `classify` pure and side-effect free in `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:31`.
Preserved existing scope-leak and loop helper structure, only changing decision thresholds/order and hang gating.
Treated an empty/default state as `Working` rather than `Initializing`, because the task’s requested priority list and acceptance criteria only define `Working` as the default.
lessons: The repo already had partial classifier behavior, but its thresholds and `ApiHang` gating did not match the current task exactly; aligning the pure classifier was safer than changing state machinery.
The codebase uses `DateTime<Utc>` rather than `Instant` for persisted state, so the correct implementation path was to preserve that clock domain while matching the requested semantics.
To make `ApiHang` match “tokens frozen” without introducing side effects, the cleanest pure check was `last_token_activity_at` age against the same hang threshold.
fields.result_summary: success - implemented classifier priority logic and added transition-focused unit coverage for saw-core
warnings: There is still older classifier coverage in `crates/saw-core/src/lib.rs:19` based on previous behavior; tests pass, but that file now contains some redundant legacy coverage alongside the new transition-focused tests.
The task text says `now: Instant`, but this codebase’s public classifier API remains `DateTime<Utc>` for consistency with persisted session state.

decisions: Used a synthetic Claude session fixture under a temporary `HOME` so the e2e test exercises the real `saw watch` CLI without depending on a live Claude session.
Covered both `--on-stuck warn` and `--on-stuck kill`, plus a non-hanging alive case to ensure no action fires when the process is healthy.
For the kill case, the test stops the target with `SIGSTOP`, confirms ApiHang detection, then resumes it with `SIGCONT` so the queued SIGINT can terminate it cleanly while keeping control of cleanup.
lessons: The ApiHang alert duration is slightly above 120s in practice, so the test should assert `>= 120s` rather than an exact second.
On this shell, `wait` can print harmless “No record of process” messages for already-reaped background jobs during cleanup.
fields.result_summary: success - added end-to-end ApiHang detection coverage
warnings: The test currently emits benign shell cleanup noise (`wait_for: No record of process ...`) even though it passes.

artifacts: Updated `src/main.rs` to load the new `tui` module
Updated `src/cmd/tui.rs` to render `MetricsPanel` and compute live CPU/I/O deltas in the TUI loop
Updated `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs` to keep existing test-loop tests passing when state is built directly from recent tool history
decisions: Kept the new widget as a dedicated module at `src/tui/widgets/metrics_panel.rs` to match the plan’s intended structure.
Used cumulative session counters in `AgentState` for tokens/tool calls/files touched so the panel can display stable session stats.
Used an 8-cell CPU bar and human-readable byte formatting to match the acceptance criteria and plan mockup.
lessons: `collect_snapshot()` only sampled one-shot process metrics, so real-time CPU/I/O panel requirements needed per-refresh delta sampling in the TUI loop.
Existing classifier/watch tests relied on fallback test-loop detection from `recent_tool_calls`, so adding aggregate counters required preserving that path.
warnings: None.

artifacts: Added test loop state to `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:287`-`292` with `consecutive_test_failures`, `last_test_command`, and `last_test_file`
detect test commands (`cargo test`, `npm test`, `pytest`, `go test`)
parse failure signals from tool results (`is_error`, `stderr`, failure text patterns)
reset on non-test commands, successful tests, different-file edits, session start, and compact boundaries
Updated classifier logic in `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:61`-`73` to emit `AgentPhase::TestLoop { command, failure_count }` after 3 consecutive failures
decisions: Kept `compute_loop_score` for windowed heuristics, but made classifier prefer stateful test-loop tracking for reset semantics that depend on tool-result interpretation and edit context
Used `last_test_command` as the authoritative command for emitted `TestLoop` payloads
Preserved `last_test_file` on successful tests while resetting only the failure counter, so future different-file edits can still break the loop context cleanly
lessons: `recent_tool_calls` alone was not enough to satisfy the requested reset semantics; those needed to be tracked in `AgentState::apply(...)`
Matching tool results back to the latest same-sidechain call was enough for this repo’s session model without adding tool IDs to state
fields.result_summary: success - implemented classifier-backed test loop tracking with stateful consecutive failure detection and reset behavior
warnings: `cargo test --test loop_detector_tests` surfaced an existing unrelated warning: unused import `normalize_guard_paths` in `src/cmd/tui.rs:2`

artifacts: `src/cmd/watch.rs:16` — replaced nested snapshot-style `--robot` output with flat JSONL event records using `serde` serialization
`src/cmd/watch.rs:130` — `--robot` now runs as a 5s heartbeat loop and emits phase changes plus event-specific records
`src/cmd/watch.rs:276` — context reset summary length is derived from compact-summary records in the session JSONL
`src/cmd/watch.rs:654` — added robot JSON schema tests for phase change, scope leak, tool loop, test loop, context reset, heartbeat, and ISO timestamps
decisions: Kept human `saw watch` behavior one-shot and only changed `--robot` into streaming mode to minimize blast radius.
Emitted one flat JSON object per line with `#[serde(tag = "event")]` so `jq` can filter directly on `.event` and other top-level fields.
Included `test_loop` in the robot schema alongside the explicitly listed events because it is already a first-class watch alert type in this codebase.
lessons: Existing `watch` had already drifted from the plan: it was a one-shot snapshot command with a nested `snapshot` payload, so meeting the bead required promoting it into a real streaming robot loop.
Compact summaries are available in session logs via `isCompactSummary`, so `summary_length` can be computed without inventing new state.
warnings: `cargo test --workspace` passes, but there are pre-existing warnings unrelated to this task:
unused `TOOL_LOOP_WINDOW_SECS` in `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:15`
unused `AgentState` import in `src/cmd/tui.rs:21`

decisions: Added `scope_violation_count` to `AgentState` and incremented it from both write-tool and file-watcher events so the guard bar can show accurate totals.

Changed `load_state`/`collect_snapshot` to apply normalized guards before replaying session events, ensuring violation counts are calculated for the currently selected guard path(s), including after pressing `g`.

Implemented the “hover/select” detail as a dedicated second line in the guard panel plus an edit dialog that also shows the last violated file while editing, since ratatui doesn’t provide mouse hover semantics in the current TUI.
lessons: Guard violation totals needed to be recomputed against the active guard set when loading snapshots; persisting only the latest violating file was not enough for the TUI acceptance criteria.
The workspace still has an unrelated `tokio::select!` compile issue in `crates/saw-daemon/src/jsonl_tail.rs`, so validation had to rely on `cargo test -p saw --bin saw` and `cargo test -p saw-core`.
fields.result_summary: success - implemented TUI guard indicator with live violation count and interactive guard editing
warnings: The TUI accepts comma-separated guard paths in the `g` dialog; it does not yet provide per-path cursor navigation or a richer selector widget.
Full-workspace `cargo check`/`cargo build` still fail because of the pre-existing daemon `tokio::select!` issue, not because of this TUI change.

artifacts: `crates/saw-daemon/src/state_machine.rs:47` — `apply(event: AgentEvent) -> Option<AgentPhase>` updates state, runs classifier at event timestamp, stores `state.phase`, and returns phase changes
`crates/saw-daemon/src/state_machine.rs:170` — tests for tool result error propagation and `TestLoop`
decisions: Reused `AgentState::apply` instead of duplicating event mutation logic in the daemon.
Treated a phase change as full `AgentPhase` inequality, not just variant-kind change, so duration/count updates can also surface when needed.
Kept `StateMachine` minimal: constructor, state accessors, config accessors, `classify_at`, and `apply`.
lessons: The low-level event mutation logic already lived in `saw-core` via `AgentState::apply`, so the daemon state machine only needed to orchestrate atomic apply + classify + phase-change reporting.
Running classification at `event.timestamp()` keeps replay deterministic for persisted event streams.
warnings: The acceptance criteria’s per-event field updates are satisfied through `saw-core::AgentState::apply`; the daemon state machine delegates to that existing implementation rather than reimplementing each branch.
I only ran targeted tests for the new state machine path, not the full workspace suite.

artifacts: Updated `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs` to track recent file writes per path with a 5-minute window and reset state when a different file is modified.
Updated `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs` to classify `ToolLoop { file, count, since }` from `FileModified`-driven state first, with fallback to existing tool-call scoring.
decisions: Kept the existing `compute_loop_score` path as fallback for older/tool-call-only state, but prioritized `AgentState` file-write tracking when recent watcher data exists.
Stored loop window data in non-serialized `AgentState` fields (`#[serde(skip, default)]`) so checkpoints remain compatible while live classification gets accurate timing.
lessons: `FileModified` is the right source of truth for rewrite-loop detection because it captures watcher-observed rewrites and supports reset-on-different-file behavior cleanly.
`Instant` is suitable for sliding-window pruning, but reporting still needs paired wall-clock timestamps for `since`.
warnings: `cargo test --test loop_detector_tests` still shows an existing unrelated warning: `find_session_jsonl` is never used in `src/cmd/common.rs:294`.

artifacts: Added `dead_after` classifier threshold and Dead-state logic in `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:12`, `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:110`
Dead now requires `silence > 300s`, `cpu < 0.5%`, stale JSONL, frozen tokens, and flat memory; process exit still maps to `Dead` in `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:111`
Added `last_memory_activity_at` tracking from process metrics in `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:275`, `crates/saw-core/src/types.rs:817`
Updated watch output to recommend `hard kill and restart the session` and include Dead details in `src/cmd/watch.rs:321`, `src/cmd/watch.rs:381`, `src/cmd/watch.rs:476`
Updated TUI alert text for Dead UI in `src/cmd/tui.rs:563`

Added unit coverage for Dead vs ApiHang thresholds in `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:411`, `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:425`, `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:439`, `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs:489`
Added watch payload test for stale Dead signals in `src/cmd/watch.rs:775`
decisions: Kept `AgentPhase::Dead` as the acceptance-criteria variant and broadened its semantics to mean “UI unresponsive or process gone.”
Dead stays higher priority than ApiHang, but only triggers after a stricter 300s threshold with CPU, token, JSONL, and memory-flat gating.
ApiHang remains the 120s waiting-on-tool condition; Dead is intentionally not gated on `awaiting_tool_result` so it can catch UI wedged states.
lessons: The repo already used `Dead` for process death, so the safest path was to extend that variant to also cover Dead UI rather than introducing a second severe state.
Tracking “memory flat” needed one extra timestamp in state; comparing successive RSS/virtual samples was enough without changing persisted payload formats.
fields.result_summary: success - implemented Dead UI detection with 5-minute inactivity threshold, distinct from ApiHang
warnings: `cargo test --bin saw ...` reports a pre-existing warning: `find_session_jsonl` is unused in `src/cmd/common.rs:300`. It does not block tests.

decisions: Kept status on the direct snapshot path instead of adding a separate daemon transport, since current shared snapshot loading already satisfies the one-shot requirement.

lessons: The existing `saw status` implementation was close, but its human output included extra session text and its JSON payload did not expose the full `AgentState`.

artifacts: Fixed scope leak fallback classification in `crates/saw-core/src/classifier.rs`

lessons: Robot mode emits a dedicated `{"event":"alert",...}` payload for alerts, not the normal status payload shape.

decisions: Added a dedicated `AlertsPanel` widget instead of inline alert text.

lessons: The existing TUI only rendered a single alert summary from phase state; combining phase + state made it possible to satisfy the “multiple alerts” requirement without changing classifier enums.
`cargo test alerts_panel --lib --bins --tests` does not work here because `saw` has no library target; `cargo test --bin saw ...` is the correct focused path.

artifacts: added `--notify idle for 5s -> polling` fallback logic
reads from last byte offset on mode switch, preserving continuity
logs when forcing polling and when auto-switching to polling
wired watch command to pass tailer options including PID
kept existing config-driven timeout/on-stuck behavior intact
decisions: Fallback activates only when:
mode is notify
no notify events have been seen for 5s
watched process is still alive
notify has not yet been confirmed working
Once a relevant notify event arrives, notify is considered healthy and automatic fallback is disabled for that run.
Polling compares file snapshot state and then reads appended bytes from the previous offset, so mode switch does not replay or drop data.
lessons: Inotify fallback should only trigger before notify is proven working; otherwise normal quiet periods would incorrectly force polling.

decisions: Used `(started_at, jsonl mtime)` as the recency key so default selection is stable and still prefers the most recently active session.

warnings: The selection prompt behavior is implemented for `saw status` when multiple cwd-matching sessions exist. `saw watch` intentionally remains non-interactive and auto-selects the most recent session per the task requirement.

The workflow uses `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` and `cargo fmt --all -- --check` instead of the narrower literal commands from the task so the workspace is checked correctly across crates

artifacts: `install.sh:1` implements a curl\|bash installer with platform detection, GitHub latest-release lookup, release download, extraction, install destination selection, checksum verification, uninstall support, and explicit permission/network errors.
`install.sh:103` defaults installs to `/usr/local/bin` when writable, otherwise `${CARGO_HOME:-$HOME/.cargo}/bin`.
`install.sh:225` supports Linux x86_64/aarch64 and macOS x86_64/aarch64 platform detection and maps them to release artifact names used by the current workflow.
`install.sh:452` falls back to `--from-source` build only after a release artifact download failure, while release lookup failures now stop with a helpful network/version message.
`install.sh:155` makes uninstall idempotent and safe to rerun.
decisions: Kept release installs as the default path and preserved `--from-source` as an explicit escape hatch.
Chose `/usr/local/bin` when writable, else Cargo bin, per task requirements.
Treated release lookup failure as a hard error with guidance, but treated missing artifact download as a source-build fallback when a version is already known.
Kept uninstall idempotent by removing from the explicitly targeted destination or both default destinations when no destination was supplied.
lessons: The current repo’s release workflow packages artifacts as `saw-<target>.tar.gz`, not `saw-<version>-<suffix>.tar.gz`, so the installer must match workflow naming, not the README example.
In this environment, `GET /repos/quangdang46/session_activity_watcher/releases/latest` returned 404, so the graceful failure path for “no published release yet” is important.
Falling back from “latest release lookup failed” into source build is misleading for curl\|bash installs; it hides release/publication issues and can fail in unrelated ways.
warnings: Full happy-path install from a published GitHub release could not be verified here because the repo currently appears to have no accessible `latest` GitHub release.

`--from-source` validation in this environment failed because cloning the repo into a temp directory did not yield a buildable checkout; that path may need separate investigation if source installs are important.
The installer now supports Linux arm64 in addition to the originally requested Linux x86_64 and macOS x86_64/arm64 because the release workflow already builds that target.

decisions: Kept the existing README and refined inaccurate sections instead of rewriting from scratch because the repo already had a near-complete document.
lessons: Verified docs against live CLI/help output and installer behavior before finalizing examples.
